This process is simpler than drug registration but has its own specific requirements — we help from ingredient analysis to obtaining a permit.
Thanks to a fast and transparent approach, your product reaches the market in minimal time.

hero.title: FAQ
hero.subtitle: Answers to the most frequently asked questions

search.noResults: Nothing found. Please contact us directly.

items.q: How long does BFQ (supplement) registration take?
items.a: BFQ (dietary supplement) registration usually takes 3-6 months. We monitor every stage of the process.

items.q: How many years is the registration certificate valid for?
items.a: In Uzbekistan, the BFQ registration certificate is usually valid for 5 years. We recommend starting re-registration 6 months before expiration.

items.q: Is a GMP certificate mandatory?
items.a: Yes, a GMP certificate is required for products intended for import. We help you meet all requirements during BFQ registration and certification.

items.q: How is re-registration carried out?
items.a: Re-registration is a process similar to initial registration, but it's often faster. The document package is updated and goes through review again.

items.q: Can multiple products be registered at the same time?
items.a: Yes, applications for several products can be submitted at once. This often saves time and costs.

items.q: Can a foreign company register on its own?
items.a: Registering pharmaceutical products in Uzbekistan requires applying through a local representative or partner company. This is exactly the role Pharm Way Group plays.

items.q: What are the main documents required for registration?
items.a: Key documents include: manufacturer's license, GMP certificate, product specification, clinical trial data (if required), packaging and label samples, and certificate of origin.

items.q: What language should the documents be in?
items.a: Documents must be submitted in Uzbek or Russian. Documents in other languages must be accompanied by a notarized translation.

items.q: Do documents need to be notarized?
items.a: Yes, most documents must be submitted with a notarized translation. We fully assist with this process as well.

items.q: Do documents have an expiration date?
items.a: Yes, most certificates and documents have a validity period. For example, a GMP certificate is usually valid for 3 years. We check that your documents are up to date.

items.q: Are electronic documents accepted?
items.a: Electronic copies may be reviewed at the initial stage, but the official process requires originals or notarized copies.

items.q: What is the cost of your services?
items.a: Pricing is determined individually, based on project complexity, product type, and service scope. You can get an exact price during a free consultation.

items.q: How is payment made?
items.a: Payment is made in stages based on the contract. Typically there's an initial payment, with the remaining amount due upon completion.

items.q: Is the consultation paid?
items.a: No, the initial consultation is completely free. We learn about your product and needs, then present a service proposal.

items.q: Can the process be completed ahead of schedule?
items.a: We always strive to finish ahead of the set deadline. The speed of government agencies also plays an important role.

items.q: What permits are needed for import?
items.a: Importing pharmaceutical products requires a registration certificate, import license, and customs documents. We help obtain all necessary permits.

items.q: Which countries can products be imported from?
items.a: Uzbekistan can import pharmaceutical products from any country in the world. The main requirement is that the product is approved and certified in its country of origin.

items.q: Are there special requirements for cold chain products?
items.a: Yes, cold chain requirements apply to biological products and certain medicines. We fully meet these requirements through specialized logistics partners.

items.q: What should be done if there's a problem at customs?
items.a: Our experienced specialists oversee customs processes from start to finish. We resolve any issues immediately as they arise.

items.q: Which countries does Pharm Way Group work with?
items.a: We primarily serve the Uzbekistan market, and also operate in Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, and Azerbaijan.

items.q: What do I need to do to become a partner?
items.a: Contact us — by phone, email, or Telegram. We'll discuss partnership opportunities during a free consultation.

items.q: How much experience does your company have?
items.a: Pharm Way Group has been operating since 2019. Over 5+ years of experience, we have successfully registered 50+ products and work with 20+ international partners.

posts.title: Registering medicines in Uzbekistan: new requirements for 2024
posts.excerpt: The Ministry of Health of Uzbekistan has introduced a number of important changes to the pharmaceutical product registration process...
posts.author: Pharm Way Group

content.text: The process of registering pharmaceutical products in Uzbekistan has changed significantly in recent years. The new rules introduced by the Ministry of Health are aimed at bringing the process closer to international standards.
Starting in 2024, a GMP certificate is mandatory for all imported medicines. Additional product safety reports are also required.

items: Step 2: Submit the application to the ministry

content.text: Preparing all documents in advance and working with an experienced partner is key to a successful registration. Pharm Way Group supports you at every stage of the process.

posts.title: How long does supplement registration take? A complete guide
posts.excerpt: Details on the process of registering dietary supplements in Uzbekistan, required documents, and timelines...
posts.author: Pharm Way Group

content.text: Registering dietary supplements in Uzbekistan is simpler than for medicines, but it has its own specific requirements.
Supplement products must include a safety certificate and composition analysis results. The absence of prohibited substances in the composition is checked separately.

content.text: With properly prepared documents, supplement registration is usually completed within 3-6 months.

content.text: Importing medicines into Uzbekistan from abroad requires working with several government agencies.
The importing company must hold an import license and a registration certificate for the product.

posts.title: Central Asia's pharmaceutical market: outlook for 2025
posts.excerpt: Growth trends in the region's pharmaceutical market, new opportunities, and key information for foreign investors...
posts.author: Pharm Way Group

content.text: The Central Asian region has shown steady growth in the pharmaceutical sector in recent years.
The markets of Uzbekistan, Kazakhstan, and Kyrgyzstan are creating new opportunities for foreign investors. Government programs aimed at developing local manufacturing also play an important role.
By 2025, the region's market is expected to become even more open and competitive.

posts.title: GMP certificate: why it matters and how to get one
posts.excerpt: What a Good Manufacturing Practice certificate means for pharmaceutical manufacturers, and the process of obtaining it...
posts.author: Pharm Way Group

content.text: A Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) certificate is an international standard confirming the quality of pharmaceutical manufacturing.
To obtain the certificate, a manufacturing facility must meet established sanitary and quality control requirements.
Without a GMP certificate, importing medicines into most markets is not possible.

posts.title: Registering medical devices: a step-by-step guide
posts.excerpt: Every stage of the state registration process for medical equipment and devices in Uzbekistan...
posts.author: Pharm Way Group

content.text: Registering medical equipment and devices in Uzbekistan follows a different procedure than medicines.
Products are divided into categories based on risk level, with separate requirements set for each.
Correct classification significantly speeds up the process.

hero.title: Certificates & Licenses
hero.subtitle: Official documents confirming our compliance with international quality standards

items.title: GMP Certificate
items.description: International certificate confirming compliance with Good Manufacturing Practice requirements.
items.details: This certificate confirms full compliance of manufacturing, storage and transportation processes for pharmaceutical products with international GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) standards. The certificate is reviewed and renewed annually.

items.title: ISO 9001:2015
items.description: Compliance of the quality management system with the international ISO 9001:2015 standard.
items.details: The ISO 9001:2015 certificate confirms that our company's quality management system is organized in accordance with customer requirements, legal regulations, and continuous improvement principles.

items.title: ISO 13485:2016
items.description: International quality management system certificate for medical devices.
items.details: ISO 13485:2016 is a specialized quality management standard applied to the manufacturing, registration, and distribution of medical devices. This certificate confirms our high level of expertise in handling medical devices.

items.title: State Registration Certificate
items.description: Official certificate granting the right to register medicinal products in the Republic of Uzbekistan.
items.details: This certificate allows our company to legally register and sell medicinal products and medical devices in the Republic of Uzbekistan. Issued by the state sanitary-epidemiological surveillance authority.

items.title: Dietary Supplement Registration Certificate
items.description: Official certificate for registration of dietary supplements.
items.details: The safety and quality of dietary supplements have been confirmed by the state sanitary-epidemiological surveillance authority, allowing the legal sale of products in the Uzbekistan market.

items.title: Pharmaceutical Activity License
items.description: State license for the import and sale of pharmaceutical products.
items.details: This license grants our company the right to import, store, and sell pharmaceutical products within Uzbekistan. The license is regularly reviewed by state authorities.
